message:
refactor(scoring): replace discrete tier scoring with continuous log-linear and exponential decay functions

Replace hand-tuned discrete match-arm scoring tiers with smooth continuous
functions that eliminate arbitrary cliff edges between score bands and
provide more granular differentiation across the full input range:

1. GitHub stars: score_github_stars()
   - Old: 7 discrete tiers (0/2/4/6/8/9/10) with hard cutoffs at
     50/200/500/1000/2000/5000
   - New: 10 * ln(1 + stars) / ln(1 + 5000), clamped to [0, 10]
   - Effect: smooth log-linear curve, e.g. 49 stars now scores 5 instead
     of 0, 999 stars scores 8 instead of 6

2. NPM downloads: score_npm_downloads()
   - Old: 5 discrete tiers (0/2/4/6/8) with cutoffs at 500/2000/10000/50000
   - New: 8 * ln(1 + downloads) / ln(1 + 50000), clamped to [0, 8]
   - Effect: 499 downloads scores 5 instead of 0, 2000 scores 6 instead of 4

3. Activity recency: score_activity()
   - Old: 6 discrete tiers (0/3/6/9/12/15) with cutoffs at
     30/90/180/365/730 days
   - New: 15 * exp(-ln(2) * days / 180) — exponential decay with
     180-day half-life
   - Effect: naturally decaying relevance, 16 days scores 14 instead of 15,
     77 days scores 11 instead of 12, graceful degradation at all ages

All test assertions updated to match the new continuous scoring outputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
